source_pr.title: dxf: fix flaky test TestNextGenMetering
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #66367

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?
as title, task executor might exit early and cause multiple unregister of meter recorder
### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: TestNextGenMetering was flaky because the disttask task executor could exit early when there were no subtasks, leading to multiple metering unregister/flush events.
failure_signature: metering final flush/unregister is triggered more than once (test expects a single gotMeterData)
root_cause_explanation: The disttask task executor has a time-based exit path when it sees no subtasks for a while. In this test, that timing can cause the metering recorder lifecycle to run more than once (multiple unregister/final flush callbacks), making assertions flaky. The fix adds a failpoint to keep the executor from exiting during the test, ensuring the metering callback happens exactly once.
fix_pattern: Stabilize async task-executor lifecycle in tests: enable a failpoint to prevent early exit when no subtasks, so metering flush/unregister occurs deterministically once.

evidence: evidence: dxf: fix flaky test TestNextGenMetering
